  "text": "Grafana's three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin) is defined and enforced across the following layers:\n\n## 1. Role Type Definition (`pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go`)\n\nThe canonical definition of `RoleType` lives in the identity package. It declares `RoleViewer`, `RoleEditor`, and `RoleAdmin` as string constants, along with a `rolePrecedence` map (None=10, Viewer=20, Editor=30, Admin=40) that powers the `Includes()` method — making Admin include Editor which includes Viewer. The `Children()` and `Parents()` helpers derive role hierarchy from precedence. These are re-exported in `pkg/services/org/model.go` as `org.RoleViewer`, `org.RoleEditor`, `org.RoleAdmin`.\n\n## 2. Role-to-Permission Mapping\n\n### Basic Role Definitions (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go`)\n`BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` creates the in-memory `RoleDTO` objects for each of the five basic roles (None, Viewer, Editor, Admin, Grafana Admin). `DeclareFixedRoles()` wires built-in fixed roles (LDAP, org users, settings, stats, users) to Grafana Admin. The `RoleRegistration.Grants` field throughout the codebase specifies which org-role tiers receive each fixed role.\n\n### API-level Fixed Role Grants (`pkg/api/accesscontrol.go`)\n`HTTPServer.declareFixedRoles()` declares all the major fixed roles the HTTP API needs and assigns them to Viewer/Editor/Admin tiers. Examples: `datasourcesExplorerRole` → Editor, `datasourcesReaderRole` → Admin (or Viewer when license enforcement is off), `annotationsReaderRole` → Viewer, `annotationsWriterRole` → Editor, `dashboardsCreatorRole` → Editor, `dashboardsWriterRole` → Admin, `orgReaderRole` → Viewer, `orgWriterRole` → Admin.\n\n### Dashboard Permission Actions (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go`)\nDefines the three-tier action sets for dashboards:\n- `DashboardViewActions`: `[dashboards:read]`\n- `DashboardEditActions`: View + `[dashboards:write, dashboards:delete]`\n- `DashboardAdminActions`: Edit + `[dashboards.permissions:read, dashboards.permissions:write]`\n`registerDashboardRoles()` seeds `fixed:dashboards:viewer/editor/admin` and grants them to Viewer/Editor/Admin respectively. `ProvideDashboardPermissions()` creates the resource permissions service with `PermissionsToActions` mapping View/Edit/Admin strings to action lists.\n\n### Folder Permission Actions (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go`)\nSimilarly defines `FolderViewActions`, `FolderEditActions`, `FolderAdminActions` (which include alerting rule and library panel actions). `registerFolderRoles()` and `ProvideFolderPermissions()` set up the equivalent three-tier action mapping for folder resources.\n\n### Dashboard/Folder Access Constants (`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go`)\nDefines the legacy `PermissionType` iota: `PERMISSION_VIEW=1`, `PERMISSION_EDIT=2`, `PERMISSION_ADMIN=4`, used in legacy ACL-based permission checks.\n\n## 3. Permission Guard Middleware\n\n### Legacy Role Guards (`pkg/middleware/auth.go` and `pkg/middleware/middleware.go`)\n`RoleAuth(roles ...org.RoleType)` in `auth.go` is the fundamental role-based gate: it checks `c.OrgRole == role` for any of the supplied roles and calls `accessForbidden()` if none match. `Auth(options)` handles signed-in and Grafana admin checks. In `middleware.go`, pre-built guards are exposed as package-level vars:\n- `ReqEditorRole = RoleAuth(org.RoleEditor, org.RoleAdmin)` — allows Editor or Admin\n- `ReqOrgAdmin = RoleAuth(org.RoleAdmin)` — allows only Admin\n- `ReqGrafanaAdmin` — requires server admin flag\n\n### RBAC Middleware (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go`)\n`Middleware(ac AccessControl)` returns a `web.Handler` factory for fine-grained RBAC guards. It calls `authorize(c, ac, user, evaluator)` which invokes `ac.Evaluate()` with scope-injected evaluators. On failure, `deny()` returns HTTP 403. `AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware()` handles cross-org authorization. `ReqHasRole()` in `accesscontrol.go` wraps `c.HasRole()` as an evaluator predicate.\n\n### AccessControl Interface and Evaluators (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go`, `evaluator.go`)\n`AccessControl.Evaluate()` is the central permission check. `EvalPermission(action, scopes...)` creates a `permissionEvaluator` that matches the user's permission map. `EvalAll`/`EvalAny` compose evaluators.\n\n### Core Implementation (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/`)\n`acimpl.AccessControl.Evaluate()` merges in-memory basic role permissions with DB-stored managed permissions. `Service.getUserPermissions()` calls `GetOrgRoles(user)` (which returns the user's role and parent roles via the precedence hierarchy) to look up basic role permissions, then fetches managed role permissions from the DB via the store.\n\n### User Role Resolution (`pkg/services/user/identity.go`)\n`SignedInUser.HasRole(role)` returns true if the user is a Grafana admin OR if `u.OrgRole.Includes(role)` — i.e., the role hierarchy check. `GetOrgRole()` returns the assigned tier.\n\n## 4. Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance\n\n### Scope Resolvers (`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go`)\nPermission inheritance is implemented through scope resolution. `GetInheritedScopes()` traverses the folder tree upward via `folderSvc.GetParents()` and returns a slice of `folders:uid:<uid>` scopes for all ancestor folders. This means a permission granted on a parent folder applies to all nested folders and dashboards.\n\n`NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()` and `NewFolderIDScopeResolver()` convert legacy folder-id scopes into UID-based scopes, appending inherited parent scopes.\n\n`NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()` and `NewDashboardIDScopeResolver()` call `resolveDashboardScope()`, which looks up the dashboard's `FolderUID` and returns the dashboard scope plus the folder scope chain (via `GetInheritedScopes()`). This is how dashboard permissions inherit from their containing folders.\n\n### Folder Service (`pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go`, `pkg/services/folder/model.go`)\nThe `Folder` struct stores `ParentUID` and `FullpathUIDs` for efficient ancestor traversal. The `Service` provides `GetParents()` used by `GetInheritedScopes()`.\n\n### Resource Permissions Store (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go`)\n`flatResourcePermission.IsInherited(scope)` returns true for managed permissions whose scope does not directly match the requested resource (i.e., they come from a parent folder). This powers the display of inherited vs. direct permissions in the UI.\n\n### Folder/Dashboard Resource Permission Services (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/`)\n`ProvideFolderPermissions()` and `ProvideDashboardPermissions()` both configure an `InheritedScopesSolver` callback that calls `GetInheritedScopes()`, wiring the inheritance mechanism into the resource permissions evaluation engine."
}

# Grafana User Permission Model Role Coverage Audit

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that define and enforce the three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin): the role-to-permission mapping, the permission guard middleware, and the folder/dashboard permission inheritance.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the compliance domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.


## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
Let me look at a few more critical files:
Now I have enough information to compile the comprehensive answer. Let me write the final `answer.json`:

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of findings:

## Grafana Three-Tier Permission Model (Viewer/Editor/Admin)

### Role-to-Permission Mapping
- **`pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go`** — Canonical `RoleType` definition with `RoleViewer`/`RoleEditor`/`RoleAdmin` constants and a `rolePrecedence` map that enables `Includes()` hierarchy (Admin⊃Editor⊃Viewer)
- **`pkg/services/org/model.go`** — Re-exports role constants as `org.RoleViewer`, `org.RoleEditor`, `org.RoleAdmin`
- **`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go`** — `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` creates in-memory role DTOs; `DeclareFixedRoles()` wires fixed roles to org tiers
- **`pkg/api/accesscontrol.go`** — `declareFixedRoles()` assigns all HTTP API roles to Viewer/Editor/Admin grants
- **`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go`** — `DashboardViewActions`, `DashboardEditActions`, `DashboardAdminActions`; `registerDashboardRoles()` seeds fixed roles per tier
- **`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go`** — Same pattern for folders with `FolderViewActions`/`EditActions`/`AdminActions`

### Permission Guard Middleware
- **`pkg/middleware/auth.go`** — `RoleAuth()` function; `Auth()` for sign-in/Grafana-admin checks
- **`pkg/middleware/middleware.go`** — Pre-built guards: `ReqEditorRole`, `ReqOrgAdmin`, `ReqGrafanaAdmin`
- **`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go`** — `Middleware()` and `AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware()` for RBAC-based fine-grained guards; `authorize()` calls `ac.Evaluate()`
- **`pkg/services/user/identity.go`** — `SignedInUser.HasRole()` uses `OrgRole.Includes()` for hierarchical role checks

### Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance
- **`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go`** — `GetInheritedScopes()` traverses ancestor folders; `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()`, `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()`, `resolveDashboardScope()` resolve permission scopes up the folder tree
- **`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go`** — Legacy `PermissionType` (VIEW/EDIT/ADMIN)
- **`pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go`** — `IsInherited()` distinguishes direct vs. parent-inherited permissions
- **`pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go`** — Provides `GetParents()` for ancestor traversal

   315→// Declare OSS roles to the accesscontrol service
   316→func DeclareFixedRoles(service Service, cfg *setting.Cfg) error {
   317→	ldapReader := RoleRegistration{
   318→		Role:   ldapReaderRole,
   319→		Grants: []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin},
   320→	}
   321→	ldapWriter := RoleRegistration{
   322→		Role:   ldapWriterRole,
   323→		Grants: []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin},
   324→	}
   325→	orgUsersReader := RoleRegistration{
   326→		Role:   orgUsersReaderRole,
   327→		Grants: []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin, string(org.RoleAdmin)},
   328→	}
   329→	orgUsersWriter := RoleRegistration{
   330→		Role:   orgUsersWriterRole,
   331→		Grants: []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin, string(org.RoleAdmin)},
   332→	}
   333→	settingsReader := RoleRegistration{
   334→		Role:   SettingsReaderRole,
   335→		Grants: []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin},
   336→	}
   337→	statsReader := RoleRegistration{
   338→		Role:   statsReaderRole,
   339→		Grants: []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin},
   340→	}
   341→	usersReader := RoleRegistration{
   342→		Role:   usersReaderRole,
   343→		Grants: []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin},
   344→	}
   345→	usersWriter := RoleRegistration{
   346→		Role:   usersWriterRole,
   347→		Grants: []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin},
   348→	}
   349→	generalAuthConfigWriter := RoleRegistration{
   350→		Role:   generalAuthConfigWriterRole,
   351→		Grants: []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin},
   352→	}
   353→	// TODO: Move to own service when implemented
   354→	authenticationConfigWriter := RoleRegistration{
   355→		Role:   authenticationConfigWriterRole,
   356→		Grants: []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin},
   357→	}
   358→
   359→	usageStatsReader := RoleRegistration{
   360→		Role:   usagestatsReaderRole,
   361→		Grants: []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin},
   362→	}
   363→
   364→	return service.DeclareFixedRoles(
   365→		ldapReader, ldapWriter, orgUsersReader, orgUsersWriter,
   366→		settingsReader, statsReader, usersReader, usersWriter,
   367→		authenticationConfigWriter, generalAuthConfigWriter, usageStatsReader,
   368→	)
   369→}
   370→
   371→func ConcatPermissions(permissions ...[]Permission) []Permission {
   372→	if permissions == nil {
   373→		return nil
   374→	}
   375→	perms := make([]Permission, 0)
   376→	for _, p := range permissions {
   377→		pCopy := make([]Permission, 0, len(p))
   378→		copy(pCopy, p)
   379→		perms = append(perms, p...)
   380→	}
   381→	return perms
   382→}
   383→
   384→// PrefixedRoleUID generates a uid from name with the same prefix.
   385→// Generated uid is 28 bytes + length of prefix: <prefix>_base64(sha1(roleName))
   386→func PrefixedRoleUID(roleName string) string {
   387→	prefix := strings.Split(roleName, ":")[0] + "_"
   388→
   389→	// #nosec G505 Used only for generating a 160 bit hash, it's not used for security purposes
   390→	hasher := sha1.New()
   391→	hasher.Write([]byte(roleName))
   392→
   393→	return fmt.Sprintf("%s%s", prefix, base64.RawURLEncoding.EncodeToString(hasher.Sum(nil)))
   394→}
   395→
   396→// ValidateFixedRole errors when a fixed role does not match expected pattern
   397→func ValidateFixedRole(role RoleDTO) error {
   398→	if !strings.HasPrefix(role.Name, FixedRolePrefix) {
   399→		return ErrFixedRolePrefixMissing
   400→	}
   401→	return nil
   402→}
   403→
   404→// ValidateBuiltInRoles errors when a built-in role does not match expected pattern
   405→func ValidateBuiltInRoles(builtInRoles []string) error {
   406→	for _, br := range builtInRoles {
   407→		if org.RoleType(br) == org.RoleNone {
   408→			return ErrNoneRoleAssignment
   409→		}
   410→		if !org.RoleType(br).IsValid() && br != RoleGrafanaAdmin {
   411→			return ErrInvalidBuiltinRole.Build(ErrInvalidBuiltinRoleData(br))
   412→		}
   413→	}
   414→	return nil
   415→}
   416→
   417→type RegistrationList struct {
   418→	mx            sync.RWMutex
   419→	registrations []RoleRegistration
   420→}
   421→
   422→func (m *RegistrationList) Append(regs ...RoleRegistration) {
   423→	m.mx.Lock()
   424→	defer m.mx.Unlock()
   425→	m.registrations = append(m.registrations, regs...)
   426→}
   427→
   428→func (m *RegistrationList) Range(f func(registration RoleRegistration) bool) {
   429→	m.mx.RLock()
   430→	defer m.mx.RUnlock()
   431→	for _, registration := range m.registrations {
   432→		if ok := f(registration); !ok {
   433→			return
   434→		}
   435→	}
   436→}
   437→
   438→func (m *RegistrationList) Slice() []RoleRegistration {
   439→	m.mx.RLock()
   440→	defer m.mx.RUnlock()
   441→	out := make([]RoleRegistration, len(m.registrations))
   442→	copy(out, m.registrations)
   443→	return out
   444→}

true,\n\t\t},\n\t\tstring(org.RoleNone): {\n\t\t\tName:        BasicRolePrefix + \"none\",\n\t\t\tUID:         BasicRoleUIDPrefix + \"none\",\n\t\t\tOrgID:       GlobalOrgID,\n\t\t\tVersion:     1,\n\t\t\tDisplayName: string(org.RoleNone),\n\t\t\tDescription: \"None role\",\n\t\t\tGroup:       \"Basic\",\n\t\t\tPermissions: []Permission{},\n\t\t\tHidden:      true,\n\t\t},\n\t\tRoleGrafanaAdmin: {\n\t\t\tName:        BasicRolePrefix + \"grafana_admin\",\n\t\t\tUID:         BasicRoleUIDPrefix + \"grafana_admin\",\n\t\t\tOrgID:     15→type Requester interface {
    16→	user.Info
    17→	claims.AuthInfo
    18→
    19→	// GetIdentityType returns the type for the requester
    20→	GetIdentityType() claims.IdentityType
    21→
    22→	// IsIdentityType returns true if identity type for requester matches any expected identity type
    23→	IsIdentityType(expected ...claims.IdentityType) bool
    24→
    25→	// FIXME: remove and use GetIdentifier instead.
    26→	// GetRawIdentifier returns only the identifier part of the UID, excluding the type
    27→	GetRawIdentifier() string
    28→
    29→	// GetID returns namespaced internalID for the entity
    30→	// Deprecated: use GetUID instead
    31→	GetID() string
    32→
    33→	// GetEmail returns the email of the active entity.
    34→	// Can be empty.
    35→	GetEmail() string
    36→
    37→	// GetInternalID returns only the identifier part of the ID, excluding the type
    38→	GetInternalID() (int64, error)
    39→
    40→	// GetIsGrafanaAdmin returns true if the user is a server admin
    41→	GetIsGrafanaAdmin() bool
    42→
    43→	// FIXME: remove and use GetUsername instead
    44→	// GetLogin returns the login of the active entity
    45→	// Can be empty.
    46→	GetLogin() string
    47→
    48→	// GetOrgID returns the ID of the active organization
    49→	GetOrgID() int64
    50→
    51→	// GetOrgRole returns the role of the active entity in the active organization.
    52→	GetOrgRole() RoleType
    53→
    54→	// GetPermissions returns the permissions of the active entity.
    55→	GetPermissions() map[string][]string
    56→
    57→	// GetGlobalPermissions returns the permissions of the active entity that are available across all organizations.
    58→	GetGlobalPermissions() map[string][]string
    59→
    60→	// DEPRECATED: GetTeams returns the teams the entity is a member of.
    61→	// Retrieve the teams from the team service instead of using this method.
    62→	GetTeams() []int64
    63→
    64→	// DEPRECATED: GetOrgName returns the name of the active organization.
    65→	// Retrieve the organization name from the organization service instead of using this method.
    66→	GetOrgName() string
    67→
    68→	// GetAuthID returns external id for entity.
    69→	GetAuthID() string
    70→
    71→	// IsAuthenticatedBy returns true if entity was authenticated by any of supplied providers.
    72→	IsAuthenticatedBy(providers ...string) bool
    73→
    74→	// IsNil returns true if the identity is nil
    75→	// FIXME: remove this method once all services are using an interface
    76→	IsNil() bool
    77→	// GetIDToken returns a signed token representing the identity that can be forwarded to plugins and external services.
    78→	GetIDToken() string
    79→
    80→	// Legacy
    81→
    82→	// HasRole returns true if the active entity has the given role in the active organization.
    83→	HasRole(role RoleType) bool
    84→	// GetCacheKey returns a unique key for the entity.
    85→	// Add an extra prefix to avoid collisions with other caches
    86→	GetCacheKey() string
    87→	// HasUniqueId returns true if the entity has a unique id
    88→	HasUniqueId() bool
    89→}
    90→
    91→// IntIdentifier converts a typeID to an int64.
    92→// Applicable for users, service accounts, api keys and renderer service.
    93→// Errors if the identifier is not initialized or if type is not recognized.
    94→func IntIdentifier(typedID string) (int64, error) {
    95→	typ, id, err := claims.ParseTypeID(typedID)
    96→	if err != nil {
    97→		return 0, err
    98→	}
    99→
   100→	return intIdentifier(typ, id, claims.TypeUser, claims.TypeAPIKey, claims.TypeServiceAccount, claims.TypeRenderService)
   101→}
   102→
   103→// UserIdentifier converts a typeID to an int64.
   104→// Errors if the identifier is not initialized or if namespace is not recognized.
   105→// Returns 0 if the type is not user or service account
   106→func UserIdentifier(typedID string) (int64, error) {
   107→	typ, id, err := claims.ParseTypeID(typedID)
   108→	if err != nil {
   109→		return 0, err
   110→	}
   111→
   112→	return intIdentifier(typ, id, claims.TypeUser, claims.TypeServiceAccount)
   113→}
   114→
   115→func intIdentifier(typ claims.IdentityType, id string, expected ...claims.IdentityType) (int64, error) {
   116→	if claims.IsIdentityType(typ, expected...) {
   117→		id, err := strconv.ParseInt(id, 10, 64)
   118→		if err != nil {
   119→			return 0, fmt.Errorf("unrecognized format for valid type %s: %w", typ, err)
   120→		}
   121→
   122→		if id < 1 {
   123→			return 0, ErrIdentifierNotInitialized
   124→		}
   125→
   126→		return id, nil
   127→	}
   128→
   129→	return 0, ErrNotIntIdentifier
   130→}
   131→
   132→// IsIDTokenExpired returns true if the ID token is expired.
   133→// If no ID token exists, returns false.
   134→func IsIDTokenExpired(requester Requester) bool {
   135→	idToken := requester.GetIDToken()
   136→	if idToken == "" {
   137→		return false
   138→	}
   139→
   140→	parsed, err := jwt.ParseSigned(idToken, []jose.SignatureAlgorithm{jose.ES256})
   141→	if err != nil {
   142→		return false
   143→	}
   144→
   145→	var claims struct {
   146→		Expiry *jwt.NumericDate `json:"exp"`
   147→	}
   148→	if err := parsed.UnsafeClaimsWithoutVerification(&claims); err != nil {
   149→		return false
   150→	}
   151→
   152→	if claims.Expiry != nil {
   153→		expiryTime := claims.Expiry.Time()
   154→		return time.Now().After(expiryTime)
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	return false
   158→}

1→package dashboardaccess
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"errors"
     5→
     6→	"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/util"

     8→
     9→type PermissionType int
    10→
    11→const (
    12→	PERMISSION_VIEW PermissionType = 1 << iota
    13→	PERMISSION_EDIT
    14→	PERMISSION_ADMIN
    15→)
    16→
    17→func (p PermissionType) String() string {
    18→	names := map[int]string{
    19→		int(PERMISSION_VIEW):  "View",
    20→		int(PERMISSION_EDIT):  "Edit",
    21→		int(PERMISSION_ADMIN): "Admin",
    22→	}
    23→	return names[int(p)]
    24→}
    25→
    26→// Typed errors
    27→var (
    28→	ErrDashboardACLInfoMissing              = errors.New("user id and team id cannot both be empty for a dashboard permission")
    29→	ErrDashboardPermissionDashboardEmpty    = errors.New("dashboard id must be greater than zero for a dashboard permission")
    30→	ErrFolderACLInfoMissing                 = errors.New("user id and team id cannot both be empty for a folder permission")
    31→	ErrFolderPermissionFolderEmpty          = errors.New("folder id must be greater than zero for a folder permission")
    32→	ErrPermissionsWithRoleNotAllowed        = errors.New("permissions cannot have both a user and team")

    36→// DashboardErr represents a dashboard error.
    37→type DashboardErr struct {
    38→	StatusCode int
    39→	Status     string
    40→	Reason     string
    41→}
    42→
    43→// Equal returns whether equal to another DashboardErr.

    45→	return o.StatusCode == e.StatusCode && o.Status == e.Status && o.Reason == e.Reason
    46→}

    50→	if e.Reason != "" {
    51→		return e.Reason
    52→	}
    53→	return "Dashboard Error"
    54→}
    55→
    56→// Body returns the error's response body, if applicable.
    57→func (e DashboardErr) Body() util.DynMap {
    58→	if e.Status == "" {
    59→		return nil
    60→	}
    61→
    62→	return util.DynMap{"status": e.Status, "message": e.Error()}

    25→var FolderViewActions = []string{dashboards.ActionFoldersRead, accesscontrol.ActionAlertingRuleRead, libraryelements.ActionLibraryPanelsRead, accesscontrol.ActionAlertingSilencesRead}

    41→func registerFolderRoles(cfg *setting.Cfg, features featuremgmt.FeatureToggles, service accesscontrol.Service) error {
    42→	if !cfg.RBAC.PermissionsWildcardSeed("folder") {
    43→		return nil
    44→	}
    45→
    46→	viewer := accesscontrol.RoleRegistration{
    47→		Role: accesscontrol.RoleDTO{
    48→			Name:        "fixed:folders:viewer",
    49→			DisplayName: "Viewer",
    50→			Description: "View all folders and dashboards.",
    51→			Group:       "Folders",
    52→			Permissions: accesscontrol.PermissionsForActions(append(getDashboardViewActions(features), FolderViewActions...), dashboards.ScopeFoldersAll),
    53→			Hidden:      true,
    54→		},
    55→		Grants: []string{"Viewer"},
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	editor := accesscontrol.RoleRegistration{
    59→		Role: accesscontrol.RoleDTO{
    60→			Name:        "fixed:folders:editor",
    61→			DisplayName: "Editor",
    62→			Description: "Edit all folders and dashboards.",
    63→			Group:       "Folders",
    64→			Permissions: accesscontrol.PermissionsForActions(append(getDashboardEditActions(features), FolderEditActions...), dashboards.ScopeFoldersAll),
    65→			Hidden:      true,
    66→		},
    67→		Grants: []string{"Editor"},
    68→	}
    69→
    70→	admin := accesscontrol.RoleRegistration{
    71→		Role: accesscontrol.RoleDTO{
    72→			Name:        "fixed:folders:admin",
    73→			DisplayName: "Admin",
    74→			Description: "Administer all folders and dashboards",
    75→			Group:       "folders",
    76→			Permissions: accesscontrol.PermissionsForActions(append(getDashboardAdminActions(features), FolderAdminActions...), dashboards.ScopeFoldersAll),
    77→			Hidden:      true,
    78→		},
    79→		Grants: []string{"Admin"},
    80→	}
    81→
    82→	return service.DeclareFixedRoles(viewer, editor, admin)
    83→}
    84→
    85→func ProvideFolderPermissions(
    86→	cfg *setting.Cfg, features featuremgmt.FeatureToggles, router routing.RouteRegister, sql db.DB, accesscontrol accesscontrol.AccessControl,
    87→	license licensing.Licensing, folderService folder.Service, service accesscontrol.Service,
    88→	teamService team.Service, userService user.Service, actionSetService resourcepermissions.ActionSetService,
    89→) (*FolderPermissionsService, error) {
    90→	if err := registerFolderRoles(cfg, features, service); err != nil {
    91→		return nil, err
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	options := resourcepermissions.Options{
    95→		Resource:          "folders",
    96→		ResourceAttribute: "uid",
    97→		ResourceValidator: func(ctx context.Context, orgID int64, resourceID string) error {
    98→			ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "accesscontrol.ossaccesscontrol.ProvideFolderPermissions.ResourceValidator")
    99→			defer span.End()
   100→
   101→			ctx, ident := identity.WithServiceIdentity(ctx, orgID)
   102→			_, err := folderService.Get(ctx, &folder.GetFolderQuery{
   103→				UID:          &resourceID,
   104→				OrgID:        orgID,
   105→				SignedInUser: ident,
   106→			})
   107→
   108→			if err != nil {
   109→				return err
   110→			}
   111→
   112→			return nil
   113→		},
   114→		InheritedScopesSolver: func(ctx context.Context, orgID int64, resourceID string) ([]string, error) {
   115→			ctx, _ = identity.WithServiceIdentity(ctx, orgID)
   116→			return dashboards.GetInheritedScopes(ctx, orgID, resourceID, folderService)
   117→		},
   118→		Assignments: resourcepermissions.Assignments{
   119→			Users:           true,
   120→			Teams:           true,
   121→			BuiltInRoles:    true,
   122→			ServiceAccounts: true,
   123→		},
   124→		PermissionsToActions: map[string][]string{
   125→			"View":  append(getDashboardViewActions(features), FolderViewActions...),
   126→			"Edit":  append(getDashboardEditActions(features), FolderEditActions...),
   127→			"Admin": append(getDashboardAdminActions(features), FolderAdminActions...),
   128→		},
   129→		ReaderRoleName: "Permission reader",
   130→		WriterRoleName: "Permission writer",
   131→		RoleGroup:      "Folders",
   132→	}
   133→	srv, err := resourcepermissions.New(cfg, options, features, router, license, accesscontrol, service, sql, teamService, userService, actionSetService)
   134→	if err != nil {
   135→		return nil, err
   136→	}
   137→	return &FolderPermissionsService{srv}, nil
   138→}
   139→

   231→// ResourcePermission is structure that holds all actions that either a team / user / builtin-role
   232→// can perform against specific resource.
   233→type ResourcePermission struct {
   234→	ID               int64
   235→	RoleName         string
   236→	Actions          []string
   237→	Scope            string
   238→	UserID           int64
   239→	UserUID          string
   240→	UserLogin        string
   241→	UserEmail        string
   242→	TeamID           int64

   253→
   254→func (p *ResourcePermission) Contains(targetActions []string) bool {
   255→	if len(p.Actions) < len(targetActions) {
   256→		return false
   257→	}
   258→
   259→	var contain = func(arr []string, s string) bool {
   260→		for _, item := range arr {
   261→			if item == s {
   262→				return true
   263→			}
   264→		}
   265→		return false
   266→	}
   267→
   268→	for _, a := range targetActions {
   269→		if !contain(p.Actions, a) {
   270→			return false
   271→		}
   272→	}
   273→
   274→	return true
   275→}

   284→type SaveExternalServiceRoleCommand struct {

   286→	ExternalServiceID string
   287→	ServiceAccountID  int64
   288→	Permissions       []Permission

   290→
   291→func (cmd *SaveExternalServiceRoleCommand) Validate() error {
   292→	if cmd.ExternalServiceID == "" {
   293→		return errors.New("external service id not specified")
   294→	}
   295→
   296→	// slugify the external service id ID for the role to have correct name and uid
   297→	cmd.ExternalServiceID = slugify.Slugify(cmd.ExternalServiceID)
   298→
   299→	// Check and deduplicate permissions
   300→	if len(cmd.Permissions) == 0 {
   301→		return errors.New("no permissions provided")
   302→	}
   303→	dedupMap := map[Permission]bool{}
   304→	dedup := make([]Permission, 0, len(cmd.Permissions))
   305→	for i := range cmd.Permissions {
   306→		if len(cmd.Permissions[i].Action) == 0 {
   307→			return fmt.Errorf("external service %v requests a permission with no Action", cmd.ExternalServiceID)
   308→		}
   309→		if dedupMap[cmd.Permissions[i]] {
   310→			continue
   311→		}
   312→		dedupMap[cmd.Permissions[i]] = true
   313→		cmd.Permissions[i].Kind, cmd.Permissions[i].Attribute, cmd.Permissions[i].Identifier = SplitScope(cmd.Permissions[i].Scope)
   314→		dedup = append(dedup, cmd.Permissions[i])
   315→	}
   316→	cmd.Permissions = dedup
   317→
   318→	if cmd.ServiceAccountID <= 0 {
   319→		return fmt.Errorf("invalid service account id %d", cmd.ServiceAccountID)
   320→	}
   321→
   322→	return nil
   323→}

   520→func BuiltInRolesWithParents(builtInRoles []string) map[string]struct{} {
   521→	res := map[string]struct{}{}
   522→
   523→	for _, br := range builtInRoles {
   524→		res[br] = struct{}{}
   525→		if br != RoleGrafanaAdmin {
   526→			for _, parent := range org.RoleType(br).Parents() {
   527→				res[string(parent)] = struct{}{}
   528→			}
   529→		}
   530→	}
   531→
   532→	return res
   533→}

   541→	EvalAll(

    85→func ProvideOSSService(
    86→	cfg *setting.Cfg, store accesscontrol.Store, actionResolver accesscontrol.ActionResolver,
    87→	cache *localcache.CacheService, features featuremgmt.FeatureToggles, tracer tracing.Tracer,
    88→	db db.DB, permRegistry permreg.PermissionRegistry, lock *serverlock.ServerLockService,
    89→) *Service {
    90→	s := &Service{
    91→		actionResolver: actionResolver,
    92→		cache:          cache,
    93→		cfg:            cfg,
    94→		features:       features,
    95→		log:            log.New("accesscontrol.service"),
    96→		roles:          accesscontrol.BuildBasicRoleDefinitions(),
    97→		store:          store,
    98→		permRegistry:   permRegistry,
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	return s
   102→}
   103→
   104→// Service is the service implementing role based access control.
   105→type Service struct {
   106→	actionResolver accesscontrol.ActionResolver
   107→	cache          *localcache.CacheService
   108→	cfg            *setting.Cfg
   109→	features       featuremgmt.FeatureToggles
   110→	log            log.Logger
   111→	registrations  accesscontrol.RegistrationList
   112→	rolesMu        sync.RWMutex
   113→	roles          map[string]*accesscontrol.RoleDTO
   114→	store          accesscontrol.Store
   115→	permRegistry   permreg.PermissionRegistry
   116→	isInitialized  bool
   117→}
   118→
   119→func (s *Service) GetUsageStats(_ context.Context) map[string]any {
   120→	return map[string]any{
   121→		"stats.oss.accesscontrol.enabled.count": 1,
   122→	}
   123→}
   124→
   125→// GetUserPermissions returns user permissions based on built-in roles
   126→func (s *Service) GetUserPermissions(ctx context.Context, user identity.Requester, options accesscontrol.Options) ([]accesscontrol.Permission, error) {
   127→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "accesscontrol.acimpl.GetUserPermissions")
   128→	defer span.End()
   129→
   130→	timer := prometheus.NewTimer(metrics.MAccessPermissionsSummary)
   131→	defer timer.ObserveDuration()
   132→
   133→	if !s.cfg.RBAC.PermissionCache || !user.HasUniqueId() {
   134→		return s.getUserPermissions(ctx, user, options)
   135→	}
   136→
   137→	return s.getCachedUserPermissions(ctx, user, options)
   138→}
   139→
   140→func (s *Service) getUserPermissions(ctx context.Context, user identity.Requester, _ accesscontrol.Options) ([]accesscontrol.Permission, error) {
   141→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "accesscontrol.acimpl.getUserPermissions")
   142→	defer span.End()
   143→
   144→	permissions := make([]accesscontrol.Permission, 0)
   145→	s.rolesMu.RLock()
   146→	for _, builtin := range accesscontrol.GetOrgRoles(user) {
   147→		if basicRole, ok := s.roles[builtin]; ok {
   148→			permissions = append(permissions, basicRole.Permissions...)
   149→		}
   150→	}
   151→	s.rolesMu.RUnlock()
   152→	permissions = append(permissions, SharedWithMeFolderPermission)
   153→
   154→	// we don't care about the error here, if this fails we get 0 and no
   155→	// permission assigned to user will be returned, only for org role.
   156→	userID, _ := identity.UserIdentifier(user.GetID())
   157→
   158→	dbPermissions, err := s.store.GetUserPermissions(ctx, accesscontrol.GetUserPermissionsQuery{
   159→		OrgID:        user.GetOrgID(),
   160→		UserID:       userID,
   161→		Roles:        accesscontrol.GetOrgRoles(user),
   162→		TeamIDs:      user.GetTeams(),
   163→		RolePrefixes: OSSRolesPrefixes,
   164→	})
   165→	if err != nil {
   166→		return nil, err
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	dbPermissions = s.actionResolver.ExpandActionSets(dbPermissions)
   170→	return append(permissions, dbPermissions...), nil
   171→}
   172→
   173→func (s *Service) getBasicRolePermissions(ctx context.Context, role string, orgID int64) ([]accesscontrol.Permission, error) {
   174→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "accesscontrol.acimpl.getBasicRolePermissions")
   175→	defer span.End()
   176→
   177→	var permissions []accesscontrol.Permission
   178→	s.rolesMu.RLock()
   179→	if basicRole, ok := s.roles[role]; ok {
   180→		permissions = basicRole.Permissions
   181→	}
   182→	s.rolesMu.RUnlock()
   183→
   184→	// Fetch managed role permissions assigned to basic roles
   185→	dbPermissions, err := s.store.GetBasicRolesPermissions(ctx, accesscontrol.GetUserPermissionsQuery{
   186→		Roles:        []string{role},
   187→		OrgID:        orgID,
   188→		RolePrefixes: OSSRolesPrefixes,
   189→	})
   190→
   191→	dbPermissions = s.actionResolver.ExpandActionSets(dbPermissions)
   192→	return append(permissions, dbPermissions...), err
   193→}
   194→
   195→func (s *Service) getTeamsPermissions(ctx context.Context, teamIDs []int64, orgID int64) (map[int64][]accesscontrol.Permission, error) {
   196→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "accesscontrol.acimpl.getTeamsPermissions")
   197→	defer span.End()
   198→
   199→	teamPermissions, err := s.store.GetTeamsPermissions(ctx, accesscontrol.GetUserPermissionsQuery{
   200→		TeamIDs:      teamIDs,
   201→		OrgID:        orgID,
   202→		RolePrefixes: OSSRolesPrefixes,
   203→	})
   204→
   205→	for teamID, permissions := range teamPermissions {
   206→		teamPermissions[teamID] = s.actionResolver.ExpandActionSets(permissions)
   207→	}
   208→
   209→	return teamPermissions, err
   210→}
   211→
   212→// Returns only permissions directly assigned to user, without basic role and team permissions
   213→func (s *Service) getUserDirectPermissions(ctx context.Context, user identity.Requester) ([]accesscontrol.Permission, error) {
   214→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "accesscontrol.acimpl.getUserDirectPermissions")
   215→	defer span.End()
   216→
   217→	var userID int64
   218→	if user.IsIdentityType(claims.TypeUser, claims.TypeServiceAccount) {
   219→		var err error
   220→		userID, err = user.GetInternalID()
   221→		if err != nil {
   222→			return nil, err
   223→		}
   224→	}
   225→
   226→	permissions, err := s.store.GetUserPermissions(ctx, accesscontrol.GetUserPermissionsQuery{
   227→		OrgID:        user.GetOrgID(),
   228→		UserID:       userID,
   229→		RolePrefixes: OSSRolesPrefixes,
   230→	})
   231→	if err != nil {
   232→		return nil, err
   233→	}
   234→
   235→	permissions = s.actionResolver.ExpandActionSets(permissions)
   236→	permissions = append(permissions, SharedWithMeFolderPermission)
   237→
   238→	return permissions, nil
   239→}

   241→func (s *Service) getCachedUserPermissions(ctx context.Context, user identity.Requester, options accesscontrol.Options) ([]accesscontrol.Permission, error) {
   242→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "accesscontrol.acimpl.getCachedUserPermissions")
   243→	defer span.End()
   244→
   245→	permissions, err := s.getCachedBasicRolesPermissions(ctx, user, options)
   246→	if err != nil {
   247→		return nil, err
   248→	}
   249→
   250→	teamsPermissions, err := s.getCachedTeamsPermissions(ctx, user, options)
   251→	if err != nil {
   252→		return nil, err
   253→	}
   254→	permissions = append(permissions, teamsPermissions...)
   255→
   256→	userManagedPermissions, err := s.getCachedUserDirectPermissions(ctx, user, options)
   257→	if err != nil {

   259→	}
   260→	permissions = append(permissions, userManagedPermissions...)
   261→	span.SetAttributes(attribute.Int("num_permissions", len(permissions)))
   262→
   263→	return permissions, nil
   264→}

   266→func (s *Service) getCachedBasicRolesPermissions(ctx context.Context, user identity.Requester, options accesscontrol.Options) ([]accesscontrol.Permission, error) {
   267→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "accesscontrol.acimpl.getCachedBasicRolesPermissions")

   170→func resolveDashboardScope(ctx context.Context, orgID int64, dashboard *Dashboard, folderSvc folder.Service) ([]string, error) {
   171→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "dashboards.resolveDashboardScope")
   172→	span.End()
   173→
   174→	var folderUID string
   175→	if dashboard.FolderUID == "" {
   176→		folderUID = ac.GeneralFolderUID
   177→	} else {
   178→		folderUID = dashboard.FolderUID
   179→	}
   180→
   181→	result, err := GetInheritedScopes(ctx, orgID, folderUID, folderSvc)
   182→	if err != nil {
   183→		return nil, err
   184→	}
   185→
   186→	result = append([]string{
   187→		ScopeDashboardsProvider.GetResourceScopeUID(dashboard.UID),
   188→		ScopeFoldersProvider.GetResourceScopeUID(folderUID),
   189→	},
   190→		result...,
   191→	)
   192→
   193→	return result, nil
   194→}
   195→
   196→func GetInheritedScopes(ctx context.Context, orgID int64, folderUID string, folderSvc folder.Service) ([]string, error) {
   197→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "dashboards.GetInheritedScopes")
   198→	span.End()
   199→
   200→	if folderUID == ac.GeneralFolderUID {
   201→		return nil, nil
   202→	}
   203→	ancestors, err := folderSvc.GetParents(ctx, folder.GetParentsQuery{
   204→		UID:   folderUID,
   205→		OrgID: orgID,
   206→	})
   207→
   208→	if err != nil {
   209→		if errors.Is(err, folder.ErrFolderNotFound) {
   210→			return nil, err
   211→		}
   212→		return nil, ac.ErrInternal.Errorf("could not retrieve folder parents: %w", err)
   213→	}
   214→
   215→	result := make([]string, 0, len(ancestors))
   216→	for _, ff := range ancestors {
   217→		result = append(result, ScopeFoldersProvider.GetResourceScopeUID(ff.UID))
   218→	}
   219→
   220→	return result, nil
   221→}

    19→)
    20→
    21→func NewStore(cfg *setting.Cfg, sql db.DB, features featuremgmt.FeatureToggles) *store {
    22→	store := &store{cfg: cfg, sql: sql, features: features}
    23→	return store
    24→}
    25→
    26→type store struct {
    27→	cfg      *setting.Cfg
    28→	sql      db.DB
    29→	features featuremgmt.FeatureToggles
    30→}
    31→
    32→type flatResourcePermission struct {
    33→	ID               int64 `xorm:"id"`
    34→	RoleName         string
    35→	Action           string
    36→	Scope            string
    37→	UserId           int64
    38→	UserUid          string
    39→	UserLogin        string
    40→	UserEmail        string
    41→	TeamId           int64
    42→	TeamUid          string
    43→	TeamEmail        string
    44→	Team             string
    45→	BuiltInRole      string
    46→	IsServiceAccount bool `xorm:"is_service_account"`
    47→	Created          time.Time
    48→	Updated          time.Time
    49→}
    50→
    51→func (p *flatResourcePermission) IsManaged(scope string) bool {
    52→	return strings.HasPrefix(p.RoleName, accesscontrol.ManagedRolePrefix) && p.Scope == scope
    53→}
    54→
    55→// IsInherited returns true for scopes from managed permissions that don't directly match the required scope
    56→// (ie, managed permissions on a parent resource)
    57→func (p *flatResourcePermission) IsInherited(scope string) bool {
    58→	return strings.HasPrefix(p.RoleName, accesscontrol.ManagedRolePrefix) && p.Scope != scope
    59→}
    60→
    61→type DeleteResourcePermissionsCmd struct {
    62→	Resource          string
    63→	ResourceAttribute string
    64→	ResourceID        string
    65→}
    66→
    67→func (s *store) DeleteResourcePermissions(ctx context.Context, orgID int64, cmd *DeleteResourcePermissionsCmd) error {
    68→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "accesscontrol.resourcepermissions.DeleteResourcePermissions")
    69→	defer span.End()
    70→
    71→	scope := accesscontrol.Scope(cmd.Resource, cmd.ResourceAttribute, cmd.ResourceID)
    72→
    73→	err := s.sql.WithTransactionalDbSession(ctx, func(sess *db.Session) error {
    74→		var permissionIDs []int64
    75→		err := sess.SQL(
    76→			"SELECT permission.id FROM permission INNER JOIN role ON permission.role_id = role.id WHERE permission.scope = ? AND role.org_id = ?",
    77→			scope, orgID).Find(&permissionIDs)
    78→		if err != nil {
    79→			return err
    80→		}

    42→	UseSessionStorageRedirect bool

    44→
    45→// Handle handles and logs error by given status.
    46→func (ctx *ReqContext) Handle(cfg *setting.Cfg, status int, title string, err error) {
    47→	data := struct {
    48→		Title     string
    49→		AppTitle  string
    50→		AppSubUrl string
    51→		ThemeType string
    52→		ErrorMsg  error
    53→	}{title, "Grafana", cfg.AppSubURL, "dark", nil}
    54→
    55→	if err != nil {
    56→		ctx.Logger.Error(title, "error", err)

    58→

    71→	resp := make(map[string]interface{})
    72→	traceID := tracing.TraceIDFromContext(ctx.Req.Context(), false)

    74→	if err != nil {

(\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/apimachinery/errutil\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/infra/log\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/infra/tracing\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/models/usertoken\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services/org\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services/user\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/setting\"\n\t\"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/web\"\n)\n\ntype ReqContext struct {\n\t*web.Context\n\t*user.SignedInUser\n\tUserToken *usertoken.UserToken\n\n\tIsSignedIn     bool\n\tIsRenderCall   bool\n\tAllowAnonymous bool\n\tSkipDSCache    bool\n\tSkipQueryCache bool\n\tLogger         log.Logger\n\tError          error\n\t// RequestNonce is a cryptographic request identifier for use with Content Security Policy.\n\tRequestNonce               string\n\tPublicDashboardAccessToken string\n\n\tPerfmonTimer   prometheus.Summary\n\tLookupTokenErr error\n\n\t// FIXME: Remove this temporary flag after the rollout of FlagUseSessionStorageForRedirection feature flag\n\t// Tracking issue for cleaning up this flag: https://github.com/grafana/identity-access-team/issues/908\n\t// UseSessionStorageRedirect is introduced to simplify the rollout of the new redirect logic\n\tUseSessionStorageRedirect bool\n}\n\n// Handle handles and logs error by given status.\nfunc (ctx *ReqContext) Handle(cfg *setting.Cfg, status int, title string, err error) {\n\tdata := struct {\n\t\tTitle     string\n\t\tAppTitle  string\n\t\tAppSubUrl string\n\t\tThemeType string\n\t\tErrorMsg  error\n\t}{title, \"Grafana\", cfg.AppSubURL, \"dark\", nil}\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tctx.Logger.Error(title, \"error\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tctx.HTML(status, cfg.ErrTemplateName, data)\n}\n\nfunc (ctx *ReqContext) IsApiRequest() bool {\n\treturn strings.HasPrefix(ctx.Req.URL.Path, \"/api\")\n}\n\nfunc (ctx *ReqContext) IsPublicDashboardView() bool {\n\treturn ctx.PublicDashboardAccessToken != \"\"\n}\n\nfunc (ctx *ReqContext) JsonApiErr(status int, message string, err error) {\n\tresp := make(map[string]interface{})\n\ttraceID := tracing.TraceIDFromContext(ctx.Req.Context(), false)\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tresp[\"traceID\"] = traceID\n\t\tif status == http.StatusInternalServerError {\n\t\t\tctx.Logger.Error(message, \"error\", err, \"traceID\", traceID)\n\t\t} else   "text": "Grafana's three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin) is defined and enforced across the following layers:\n\n## 1. Role Type Definition (`pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go`)\n\nThe canonical definition of `RoleType` lives in the identity package. It declares `RoleViewer`, `RoleEditor`, and `RoleAdmin` as string constants, along with a `rolePrecedence` map (None=10, Viewer=20, Editor=30, Admin=40) that powers the `Includes()` method — making Admin include Editor which includes Viewer. The `Children()` and `Parents()` helpers derive role hierarchy from precedence. These are re-exported in `pkg/services/org/model.go` as `org.RoleViewer`, `org.RoleEditor`, `org.RoleAdmin`.\n\n## 2. Role-to-Permission Mapping\n\n### Basic Role Definitions (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go`)\n`BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` creates the in-memory `RoleDTO` objects for each of the five basic roles (None, Viewer, Editor, Admin, Grafana Admin). `DeclareFixedRoles()` wires built-in fixed roles (LDAP, org users, settings, stats, users) to Grafana Admin. The `RoleRegistration.Grants` field throughout the codebase specifies which org-role tiers receive each fixed role.\n\n### API-level Fixed Role Grants (`pkg/api/accesscontrol.go`)\n`HTTPServer.declareFixedRoles()` declares all the major fixed roles the HTTP API needs and assigns them to Viewer/Editor/Admin tiers. Examples: `datasourcesExplorerRole` → Editor, `datasourcesReaderRole` → Admin (or Viewer when license enforcement is off), `annotationsReaderRole` → Viewer, `annotationsWriterRole` → Editor, `dashboardsCreatorRole` → Editor, `dashboardsWriterRole` → Admin, `orgReaderRole` → Viewer, `orgWriterRole` → Admin.\n\n### Dashboard Permission Actions (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go`)\nDefines the three-tier action sets for dashboards:\n- `DashboardViewActions`: `[dashboards:read]`\n- `DashboardEditActions`: View + `[dashboards:write, dashboards:delete]`\n- `DashboardAdminActions`: Edit + `[dashboards.permissions:read, dashboards.permissions:write]`\n`registerDashboardRoles()` seeds `fixed:dashboards:viewer/editor/admin` and grants them to Viewer/Editor/Admin respectively. `ProvideDashboardPermissions()` creates the resource permissions service with `PermissionsToActions` mapping View/Edit/Admin strings to action lists.\n\n### Folder Permission Actions (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go`)\nSimilarly defines `FolderViewActions`, `FolderEditActions`, `FolderAdminActions` (which include alerting rule and library panel actions). `registerFolderRoles()` and `ProvideFolderPermissions()` set up the equivalent three-tier action mapping for folder resources.\n\n### Dashboard/Folder Access Constants (`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go`)\nDefines the legacy `PermissionType` iota: `PERMISSION_VIEW=1`, `PERMISSION_EDIT=2`, `PERMISSION_ADMIN=4`, used in legacy ACL-based permission checks.\n\n## 3. Permission Guard Middleware\n\n### Legacy Role Guards (`pkg/middleware/auth.go` and `pkg/middleware/middleware.go`)\n`RoleAuth(roles ...org.RoleType)` in `auth.go` is the fundamental role-based gate: it checks `c.OrgRole == role` for any of the supplied roles and calls `accessForbidden()` if none match. `Auth(options)` handles signed-in and Grafana admin checks. In `middleware.go`, pre-built guards are exposed as package-level vars:\n- `ReqEditorRole = RoleAuth(org.RoleEditor, org.RoleAdmin)` — allows Editor or Admin\n- `ReqOrgAdmin = RoleAuth(org.RoleAdmin)` — allows only Admin\n- `ReqGrafanaAdmin` — requires server admin flag\n\n### RBAC Middleware (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go`)\n`Middleware(ac AccessControl)` returns a `web.Handler` factory for fine-grained RBAC guards. It calls `authorize(c, ac, user, evaluator)` which invokes `ac.Evaluate()` with scope-injected evaluators. On failure, `deny()` returns HTTP 403. `AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware()` handles cross-org authorization. `ReqHasRole()` in `accesscontrol.go` wraps `c.HasRole()` as an evaluator predicate.\n\n### AccessControl Interface and Evaluators (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go`, `evaluator.go`)\n`AccessControl.Evaluate()` is the central permission check. `EvalPermission(action, scopes...)` creates a `permissionEvaluator` that matches the user's permission map. `EvalAll`/`EvalAny` compose evaluators.\n\n### Core Implementation (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/`)\n`acimpl.AccessControl.Evaluate()` merges in-memory basic role permissions with DB-stored managed permissions. `Service.getUserPermissions()` calls `GetOrgRoles(user)` (which returns the user's role and parent roles via the precedence hierarchy) to look up basic role permissions, then fetches managed role permissions from the DB via the store.\n\n### User Role Resolution (`pkg/services/user/identity.go`)\n`SignedInUser.HasRole(role)` returns true if the user is a Grafana admin OR if `u.OrgRole.Includes(role)` — i.e., the role hierarchy check. `GetOrgRole()` returns the assigned tier.\n\n## 4. Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance\n\n### Scope Resolvers (`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go`)\nPermission inheritance is implemented through scope resolution. `GetInheritedScopes()` traverses the folder tree upward via `folderSvc.GetParents()` and returns a slice of `folders:uid:<uid>` scopes for all ancestor folders. This means a permission granted on a parent folder applies to all nested folders and dashboards.\n\n`NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()` and `NewFolderIDScopeResolver()` convert legacy folder-id scopes into UID-based scopes, appending inherited parent scopes.\n\n`NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()` and `NewDashboardIDScopeResolver()` call `resolveDashboardScope()`, which looks up the dashboard's `FolderUID` and returns the dashboard scope plus the folder scope chain (via `GetInheritedScopes()`). This is how dashboard permissions inherit from their containing folders.\n\n### Folder Service (`pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go`, `pkg/services/folder/model.go`)\nThe `Folder` struct stores `ParentUID` and `FullpathUIDs` for efficient ancestor traversal. The `Service` provides `GetParents()` used by `GetInheritedScopes()`.\n\n### Resource Permissions Store (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go`)\n`flatResourcePermission.IsInherited(scope)` returns true for managed permissions whose scope does not directly match the requested resource (i.e., they come from a parent folder). This powers the display of inherited vs. direct permissions in the UI.\n\n### Folder/Dashboard Resource Permission Services (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/`)\n`ProvideFolderPermissions()` and `ProvideDashboardPermissions()` both configure an `InheritedScopesSolver` callback that calls `GetInheritedScopes()`, wiring the inheritance mechanism into the resource permissions evaluation engine."
}

Role-to-Permission Mapping\n\n### Basic Role Definitions (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go`)\n`BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` creates the in-memory `RoleDTO` objects for each of the five basic roles (None, Viewer, Editor, Admin, Grafana Admin). `DeclareFixedRoles()` wires built-in fixed roles (LDAP, org users, settings, stats, users) to Grafana Admin. Examples: `datasourcesExplorerRole` → Editor, `datasourcesReaderRole` → Admin (or Viewer when license enforcement is off), `annotationsReaderRole` → Viewer, `annotationsWriterRole` → Editor, `dashboardsCreatorRole` → Editor, `dashboardsWriterRole` → Admin, `orgReaderRole` → Viewer, `orgWriterRole` → Admin.\n\n### Dashboard Permission Actions (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go`)\nDefines the three-tier action sets for dashboards:\n- `DashboardViewActions`: `[dashboards:read]`\n- `DashboardEditActions`: View + `[dashboards:write, dashboards:delete]`\n- `DashboardAdminActions`: Edit + `[dashboards.permissions:read, dashboards.permissions:write]`\n`registerDashboardRoles()` seeds `fixed:dashboards:viewer/editor/admin` and grants them to Viewer/Editor/Admin respectively. `ProvideDashboardPermissions()` creates the resource permissions service with `PermissionsToActions` mapping View/Edit/Admin strings to action lists.\n\n### Folder Permission Actions (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go`)\nSimilarly defines `FolderViewActions`, `FolderEditActions`, `FolderAdminActions` (which include alerting rule and library panel actions). `registerFolderRoles()` and `ProvideFolderPermissions()` set up the equivalent three-tier action mapping for folder resources.\n\n### Dashboard/Folder Access Constants (`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go`)\nDefines the legacy `PermissionType` iota: `PERMISSION_VIEW=1`, `PERMISSION_EDIT=2`, `PERMISSION_ADMIN=4`, used in legacy ACL-based permission checks.\n\n## 3. Permission Guard Middleware\n\n### Legacy Role Guards (`pkg/middleware/auth.go` and `pkg/middleware/middleware.go`)\n`RoleAuth(roles ...org.RoleType)` in `auth.go` is the fundamental role-based gate: it checks `c.OrgRole == role` for any of the supplied roles and calls `accessForbidden()` if none match. `Auth(options)` handles signed-in and Grafana admin checks. In `middleware.go`, pre-built guards are exposed as package-level vars:\n- `ReqEditorRole = RoleAuth(org.RoleEditor, org.RoleAdmin)` — allows Editor or Admin\n- `ReqOrgAdmin = RoleAuth(org.RoleAdmin)` — allows only Admin\n- `ReqGrafanaAdmin` — requires server admin flag\n\n### RBAC Middleware (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go`)\n`Middleware(ac AccessControl)` returns a `web.Handler` factory for fine-grained RBAC guards. It calls `authorize(c, ac, user, evaluator)` which invokes `ac.Evaluate()` with scope-injected evaluators. On failure, `deny()` returns HTTP 403. `AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware()` handles cross-org authorization. `ReqHasRole()` in `accesscontrol.go` wraps `c.HasRole()` as an evaluator predicate.\n\n### AccessControl Interface and Evaluators (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go`, `evaluator.go`)\n`AccessControl.Evaluate()` is the central permission check. `EvalPermission(action, scopes...)` creates a `permissionEvaluator` that matches the user's permission map. `EvalAll`/`EvalAny` compose evaluators.\n\n### Core Implementation (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/`)\n`acimpl.AccessControl.Evaluate()` merges in-memory basic role permissions with DB-stored managed permissions. `Service.getUserPermissions()` calls `GetOrgRoles(user)` (which returns the user's role and parent roles via the precedence hierarchy) to look up basic role permissions, then fetches managed role permissions from the DB via the store.\n\n### User Role Resolution (`pkg/services/user/identity.go`)\n`SignedInUser.HasRole(role)` returns true if the user is a Grafana admin OR if `u.OrgRole.Includes(role)` — i.e., the role hierarchy check. `GetOrgRole()` returns the assigned tier.\n\n## 4. Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance\n\n### Scope Resolvers (`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go`)\nPermission inheritance is implemented through scope resolution. `GetInheritedScopes()` traverses the folder tree upward via `folderSvc.GetParents()` and returns a slice of `folders:uid:<uid>` scopes for all ancestor folders. This means a permission granted on a parent folder applies to all nested folders and dashboards.\n\n`NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()` and `NewFolderIDScopeResolver()` convert legacy folder-id scopes into UID-based scopes, appending inherited parent scopes.\n\n`NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()` and `NewDashboardIDScopeResolver()` call `resolveDashboardScope()`, which looks up the dashboard's `FolderUID` and returns the dashboard scope plus the folder scope chain (via `GetInheritedScopes()`). This is how dashboard permissions inherit from their containing folders.\n\n### Folder Service (`pkg/services/folder/folderimpl/folder.go`, `pkg/services/folder/model.go`)\nThe `Folder` struct stores `ParentUID` and `FullpathUIDs` for efficient ancestor traversal. The `Service` provides `GetParents()` used by `GetInheritedScopes()`.\n\n### Resource Permissions Store (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go`)\n`flatResourcePermission.IsInherited(scope)` returns true for managed permissions whose scope does not directly match the requested resource (i.e., they come from a parent folder). This powers the display of inherited vs. direct permissions in the UI.\n\n### Folder/Dashboard Resource Permission Services (`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/`)\n`ProvideFolderPermissions()` and `ProvideDashboardPermissions()` both configure an `InheritedScopesSolver` callback that calls `GetInheritedScopes()`, wiring the inheritance mechanism into the resource permissions evaluation engine."
}
